assumptions: secp256k1 ECDSA is EUF-CMA secure under canonical (low-s) signing
Block timestamps cannot drift more than 15 seconds (per Ethereum consensus)
Validator set rotation increments the validatorEpoch monotonically
FRESHNESS_WINDOW = 3600 seconds is consensus-appropriate; longer windows would weaken A5

walkaway.explanation: PatchedBridge has no upgrade path or admin function; validator set is established at construction. Validator rotation requires a NEW deployment with new epoch, not an in-place admin operation. The bridge does not have a 'pause' or 'drain' admin function. Classified bounded because the bridge depends on the validator set's continued existence (off-chain attestation infrastructure), but on-chain there is no irreplaceable role.
dependencies_disclosed: Off-chain validator infrastructure must continue to attest withdrawals; if all validators disappear, deposits cannot be withdrawn (liveness concern, not safety)

evidence.justification: Event emission after external call; reorderable. Weaker temporal-distinctness violation but flagged for completeness.

Lean 4 proof in demos/bridge/proof/Attestation.lean establishes A3 (signature integrity) for PatchedBridge. Theorem patched_rejects_high_s shows that high-s signatures are rejected by the verifier, foreclosing the signature-malleability replay family. Zero sorry.

Lean 4 proofs in demos/bridge/proof/Attestation.lean (stale_attestation_rejected, future_attestation_rejected, epoch_rotation_breaks_replay) establish A5 (external attestation freshness). The bridge rejects stale and future attestations, and epoch rotation breaks replay across validator-set changes.

PatchedBridge has no admin role; the walkaway property holds at bounded level per the bounded-walkaway lemma in lean/Parallax5/Walkaway.lean. Bounded rather than full because the bridge depends on off-chain validator infrastructure for liveness.
